primary_findings: Gemma3 model semantics are broadly aligned between vLLM and Ferrum.
Existing native probes rule out dense Marlin kernel selection as the main c16 gap.
The remaining source-level gap is decode integration: vLLM uses persistent buffers and CUDA graph dispatch, while Ferrum's usable Gemma3 unified path remains eager.
Ferrum unified graph is the closest matching lever but is currently not correctness-clean under c16 diagnostics.
next_minimal_validation: Target unified-graph correctness/memory safety for one Gemma3 unified decode shape before any broader performance sweep.
